citations.finding: exposure-response is concave and hashtag-dependent; not linear complex contagion universally
citations.limits: Twitter only; hashtag adoption, not general behavior

exposure_response_hazard.formal_description: λ_i(t)=exp(θ·x), x=[1,log1p(k_i),log1p(deg_i),k_i/deg_i,exp(-recency/24)]; window activation P=1-exp(-∫λ dt), integrated event-by-event over the cohort subgraph with in-sample exposure growth (rollout).
known_failure_modes: rollout adds nothing when in-sample subgraph is sparse (honest scope)
exposure_response_hazard.title: Nonlinear log-linear exposure-response activation hazard
exposure_response_hazard.uncertainty_note: per-particle lognormal hazard-scale draw (parameter uncertainty); frailty sd fitted by profile likelihood

hawkes_self_excitation.formal_description: λ(t)=μ+αω·Σ_{t_i<t} exp(-ω(t-t_i)); exponential kernel, EM/MLE fit
known_failure_modes: constant μ + single exponential kernel underfit the Higgs activity burst
hawkes_self_excitation.status_reason: held-out count forecast FAILED to beat Poisson on the Higgs stream; preserved
hawkes_self_excitation.title: Hawkes self-exciting point process

social_preference_population.formal_description: u=x_own-α·max(x_other-x_own,0)-β·max(x_own-x_other,0); discrete (α,β) type mixture, weights fitted jointly across games (partial pooling); drives every two-player game through one utility model; cross-game interaction via shared responder-threshold / banker-return beliefs.
known_failure_modes: public_goods badly misfit (W1 0.292, KS 0.458) — FS→PG mapping too crude
does not beat per-game fitted histogram in-distribution
social_preference_population.title: Fehr-Schmidt inequity-aversion population mixture
social_preference_population.uncertainty_note: per-particle type draw from the fitted mixture
